description: Conception Participation aux réunions fonctionnelles avec le Product Owner et autres parties prenantes Garantie de l'homogénéité des développements et de l'architecture en relation avec les autres équipes de la R&D Réalisation des spécifications techniques et participation aux analyses conceptuelles Chiffrage des charges de développement Développement Réalisation du développement, qualité de code Implémentation des tests unitaires Référent auprès que QA intégré à l’équipe de dev Participation aux différentes cérémonies d’un sprint Scrum : Daily Meeting, Sprint Review, etc. Production de la documentation technique du logiciel Rédaction et vérification des manuels d'utilisation et d'installation Reporting (dérives, problèmes techniques, ...) à l’équipe et autres parties prenantes Divers Formation et assistance aux services tels que : pôles expertise, intégration et support Assure la veille technologique Coordination entre l’équipe de développement et le Product Owner Reporting régulier de l'activité au Manager

title: Alternant consultant Transformation Organisationnelle F/H
description: Digital Services est l' ESN d'Orange Business qui combine les expertises Cloud, Digital, Data et IA. Plus de 4 000 experts répartis dans toute la France accompagnent des entreprises et institutions publiques dans leur transformation numérique. Nous rejoindre, c'est participer à l'accompagnement de nos clients à travers des missions stimulantes et variées. 🟨 Au sein de l’équipe Design vous accompagnerez l’équipe sur des problématiques de transformation organisationnelle, d’évolution des modèles de fonctionnement et d’amélioration de l’expérience collaborateurs et clients. 🟨 Vos missions seront les suivantes : - Contribution aux projets de transformation • Participer aux phases de cadrage : compréhension des enjeux stratégiques, analyse de l’existant, identification des irritants organisationnels • Réaliser des diagnostics (parcours, processus, interactions, gouvernance, modes de collaboration) • Contribuer à la modélisation de situations actuelles et cibles (blueprints, operating models simplifiés, chaînes de valeur) • Aider à la formulation de recommandations structurantes et actionnables - Facilitation et intelligence collective • Participer à la conception et à l’animation d’ateliers avec des parties prenantes variées (métiers, fonctions support, direction…) • Produire des supports favorisant l’alignement et la prise de décision • Accompagner les dynamiques de co-construction - Structuration d’une offre de transformation • Contribuer à la formalisation de notre proposition de valeur • Réaliser des benchmarks sur les tendances en transformation des organisations • Participer à la création de supports commerciaux et méthodologiques • Aider à clarifier nos approches (cadres d’intervention, livrables, narration) - Développement de la posture de consultant • Apprendre à naviguer dans des environnements complexes • Développer une pensée structurée et systémique • Renforcer sa capacité à problématiser • Gagner en impact à l’oral comme à l’écrit

title: Alternant Business Manager F/H
description: Digital Services est l' ESN d'Orange Business qui combine les expertises Cloud, Digital, Data et IA. Plus de 4 000 experts répartis dans toute la France accompagnent des entreprises et institutions publiques dans leur transformation numérique. Nous rejoindre, c'est participer à l'accompagnement de nos clients à travers des missions stimulantes et variées. 🟨Nous recherchons un(e) alternant(e) Business Manager pour notre équipe nantaise. Rattaché(e) à l’une de nos agences sectorielles : Banque Assurance, dans une équipe dynamique et expérimentée, vous intervenez dans le cadre de projets de transformation digitale, en tant que développeur commercial, auprès de prospects et client privés ou publics sur les territoires Grand-Ouest et Grands Comptes Nationaux. 🟨Dans le cadre du développement de nos offres de prestation de service IT en région & de la solution DXP Liferay au niveau national, vos missions seront les suivantes : Participer à l’élaboration de la stratégie de développement du CA sur votre périmètre Analyser les métiers et l'environnement de vos clients : stratégie, écosystème, interlocuteurs stratégiques (DG, DSI, RH, marketing, commercial), en identifiant les enjeux du client et leurs impacts sur leurs activités Démarcher nos prospects et clients dans la région et sur des grands comptes nationaux Proposer nos solutions (Produit et service) dans le but de générer des leads commerciaux Suivre le pipeline commercial et son développement marketing avec l’Editeur Animer les leads et accompagner les vendeurs sur les dernières briques technologiques Participer activement à l'animation commerciale interne et externe.

title: Production Operator Advanced - Meals
description: The Meals department are keen to promote excellent team work by employing people who have a positive attitude, who are prepared to learn a number of skills so that they are able to work in various positions within the department. By achieving this, the department is in the best position to produce quality products, in an efficient manner. We aim to not only achieve our targets but improve on them as well. The working hours for this position are Monday to Friday 14.00 – 22.00 on a 40 hour week. £13.40 per hour (£13.80 from 1st May 2026) + Bonus + Benefits

title: Manufacturing Supervisor
description: Manufacturing Supervisor – Meals 40 hours per week | Monday - Friday, 6am–2pm The Meals Supervisor role is an important addition to the Meals Department which is being introduced due to our expanded production volumes. The Supervisor is responsible for overseeing several production lines; ensuring these lines are performing efficiently to produce the high volume and quality of product needed. You will involve work with the Line Leaders to understand performance of their lines and troubleshoot any issues- mechanical, material, or staff, then working with and supporting them to resolve these. We are looking for someone with experience in supervising and leading teams in a manufacturing setting. You'll need experience planning the run, understanding of Key Performance Indicators, and the ability to motivating staff. Main purpose of the job: To ensure that the department runs to its maximum efficiency with right first time quality productivity. You will assist in achieving this objective by managing 4 lines to ensure they perform to the best of their ability. Cover: To cover the Shift Managers’ role during Holiday, Absence and Training Staff are trained to be multi-functional and to be capable of carrying out numerous functions if needed.

title: Depot Operations Supervisor
description: The Depot Operations Supervisor reports to the Retail Sales & Operations Manager (RSOM) who has overall responsibility for the depot and service across: Customer Services Delivery fleet Cold store Warehouse This role is 40 hours per week, working any 5 of 6 days (closed Sundays). We offer a competitive hourly rate of £14.94 per hour.

title: Office Supervisor
description: 37.5 days per week, working 5 out of 6 days. The new Office Supervisor for Wiltshire Farm Foods Norfolk must be passionate and have the energy and commitment to support the Retail Sales and Operations Manager to create an exemplar of outstanding performance in customer service and sales . As Office Supervisor you'll supervise the office based team to deliver exceptional service to our customers via phone and and email. Customer service and empathy for the needs of our predominately elderly customers, is at the heart of what we do and you'll need to be part of living those values. The WFF network is actively supported with national marketing, including television, national press and direct mail marketing campaigns. You can also tap into our support from our Wiltshire Head Office. £14.94 per hour + Bonus + Benefits 37.5 days per week, working 5 out of 6 days.

title: Meals Support Key Operator
description: This role is 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday working 06:00-14:00 shift. The Meals department is keen to promote excellent team work by employing people who have a positive (can do) attitude. By achieving this, the department is in the best position to produce products in an efficient manner. We aim to not only achieve our targets but improve on them as well. The successful candidate will ensure that the m eals support areas, meat slicing, de-canning & packaging department runs to its maximum efficiency. Staff are employed and trained to be multi-functional and to be capable of carrying out numerous functions should this be required when a fellow staff member is away or absent. £15.93 per hour + Bonus + Benefits

title: Production Operator - Meat Slicing
description: This role is 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday working 06:00am – 14:00pm. The Meals department are keen to promote excellent team work by employing people who have a positive (can do) attitude, who are prepared to learn a number of skills so that they are able to work in various positions within the department. By achieving this, the department is in the best position to produce quality products, in an efficient manner. We aim to not only achieve our targets but improve on them as well. The successful candidate will need to be a key member of the production team slicing meat to the correct weight specification in a safe and hygienic manner. £14.09 per hour + Bonus + Benefits

title: Customer Service Advisor
description: This role is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days The role of our Customer Services Advisor is critical to the success of our business. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. €15.50 per hour + bonus + company benefits.

title: Key Operator - High Care Kitchen
description: This position is 40 hours per week on 14.00 - 22.00 Shift pattern. The High Care Kitchen department decants cooked fills from the low risk kitchen to directly supply the meals and sweets department. Produce, vegetables and fillings are blast chilled to CCP temperatures and held in the chiller for meals use and transport to the Pie Site. Vegetables are minced from chilled cooked and frozen varieties are mixed together with dry/wet seasoning using a tumbler machine. Fresh mashed potato is cooked within the department for direct use to the meals department and ranges of soups and sauces is prepared also. To organise and aid your team to supply mashed potato, cooked fills and vegetables to 3 production areas; meals, sweets and the pie department. This must be done with the products being fit for use and on time to aid production plans and targets with minimal downtime and cost of devaluation of goods. The post holder will work with the operators to follow the machine plans set by the kitchen TL. Hourly targets are set to the tumbler, Stephan and mash stations and these targets must be met to achieve production to the supply areas. These hourly targets have been historically set and is the one of the KPIs within the kitchen area i.e. 6 mixes per hour for any mixed vegetable, 600kg of minced soft diet vegetable can be made per hour on the Stephan machine. £15.93 per hour + Shift Allowance + Bonus + Benefits

title: Class C + E Driver (Nights) - Yard Shunter
description: This role is working part-time, 36 hours per week, working 3 in 7. apetito is the UK’s leading supplier of food and catering solutions to care homes, local authorities & hospitals and the transport and distribution area of the business is a critically important part of our supply chain. Our drivers are the key link between our business and the customers we work with; ensuring our work is delivered professionally, efficiently and on time. As a professional driver, this role represents a chance to join a growing and forward-thinking business as an ambassador for apetito delivering our finished frozen food product to Care Homes, Local Authorities, Hospitals and our Wiltshire Farm Foods franchisees.  In this critical role you must be prepared to drive to different geographical locations and manage a demanding delivery schedule – our customers depend on our food and we depend on you. Additionally, you will need to ensure that you provide fantastic customer service as the face of apetito The transport and distribution area of the business is a critical and important part of the supply chain. Our main base is Portbury near Bristol, but we also have staff at our out base depots in the following locations; Stanmore, Gillingham, South Kirkby, St Helens and Motherwell in Scotland. £37,869.42 per annum + Overtime + Bonus + Benefits

description: This position is working 40 hours per week, any 5 in 6 days including bank holidays and Saturdays Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you may be asked to cover coldstore holidays. It runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. £13.45 per hour + bonus + company benefits.

title: Peer Recovery Coach, Day Shift, Behavioral Health
description: AHC SGMC Behavioral Health If you are a current Adventist HealthCare employee, please click this link to apply through your Workday account. Shady Grove Medical Center - Behavioral Health seeks to hire an experienced Peer Recovery Coach who will embrace our mission to extend God’s care through the ministry of physical, mental, and spiritual healing. As a Peer Recovery Coach you will: • Conducts brief interventions to patients that screen positive for alcohol and/or drug use. • Identifies patient’s history of drug/alcohol use, treatment history, motivation to change behavior and other needs for community support services. • Identifies patient’s readiness to change and use motivational interviewing techniques and other communication skills to support a plan for reducing or eliminating harmful drug and/or alcohol use. • Works with ED staff and social worker to identify community support services. • Assist patient and family with understanding how to utilize these community services. • Assists patients in linking to community support services and substance treatment services including securing appointment and transportation. • Acts as a peer support throughout the hospitalization and post-discharge, as appropriate to facilitate attainment of goals. • Provides family members with recovery support materials. • Provides telephone outreach to patients to assist with linkages and continued peer support. • Models coping techniques and self-help strategies. • Demonstrates ability to meet the productivity requirements set in accordance with program goals. • Participates in trainings to continually gain new skills. • Attends required staff and other meetings. • Completes required documentation and other reports. • Acts as a resource to other clinical team members on recovery support. • Consults with nursing staff, social work as requested, even if patient does not screen positive. • Notifies patient’s nurse before visiting the patient. • Telephones treatment providers to verify patient showed up for appointment; and follow-up by telephone with any patient that misses an appointment. • Demonstrates competencies for position within the first 90 days of employment. • Satisfactorily meets requirements of position during periodic and annual evaluations. Qualifications include: • High school diploma or G.E.D. is required. • The candidate must be actively engaged in his/her own recovery program with a minimum of three years demonstrated personal recovery and sustained • abstinence from alcohol and/or drug use. • For PHP roles, may accept mental health recovery experience in place of substance abuse recovery experience. Work Schedule: Employment Type: Full-time Hours per Week: 40 hrs/week Typical Daily Schedule: 8:30 AM–5:00 PM Monday through Friday Shift Type: Day Holiday Requirements: Follow company holiday calendar Shift Differential Eligible: Yes Pay Range: $19.21 - $24.90 If the salary range is listed as $0 or if the position is Per Diem (with a fixed rate), salary discussions will take place during the screening process. Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), this position is classified as: United States of America (Non-Exempt) At Adventist HealthCare our job is to care for you. We do this by offering: Work life balance through nonrotating shifts Recognition and rewards for professional expertise Free Employee parking Medical, Prescription, Dental, and Vision coverage for employees and their eligible dependents effective on your date of hire Employer-paid Short & Long-Term Disability, Basic Life Insurance and AD&D, (short-term disability buy-up available) Paid Time Off Employer retirement contribution and match after 1-year of eligible employment with a 3-year vesting period Voluntary benefits include flexible spending accounts, legal plans, and life, pet, auto, home, long term care, and critical illness & accident insurance Subsidized childcare at participating childcare centers Tuition Reimbursement Employee Assistance Program (EAP) support As a faith-based organization, with over a century of caring for the communities in the Maryland area, Adventist HealthCare has earned a reputation for high-quality, compassionate care. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). description: JOB DESCRIPTION: About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Mission Statement To serve as a compelling opportunity for early‑career professionals, attracting top talent to Abbott and accelerating their progression into the leadership pipeline through experiential learning, structured training, and performance-driven development. Program Highlights Operations PDP (OPDP) begin their careers at Abbott through a three-year program designed to provide broad growth and development, combining both breadth and depth of Operations experience. Upon completion, PDP participants transition into their next full-time roles, continuing to grow their careers and contribute as Abbott professionals, supported by the strong foundation of skills, experience, and network built during the program. Program Overview Over the course of three years, PDP participants engage in three critical career experiences designed to accelerate learning and development. PDP participants complete three distinct assignments across different divisions and/or functional areas, including Technical, Supervisory, and Operations Excellence roles. The program provides diverse development opportunities through rotations in areas such as Production, Engineering, Quality, Project Management, and Operations Excellence. Rotation Guidelines PDP participants complete three assignments (12 months each) across different divisions and/or functional areas. Each assignment is unique and conducted under a different Assignment Manager, no assignment is repeated. PDP participants starting in July 2027 will complete the three‑year program by June 2030, with the final rotation being an international (overseas) assignment. description: Join our national Building Performance certification team - bring your sustainability project leadership expertise for embodied carbon to HGA! Locations: Minneapolis, MN; Milwaukee or Madison; WI; Boston, MA; Washington, DC; Sacramento, San Francisco, or Los Angeles, CA As demand for sustainable, high‑performance environments continues to rise, our work increasingly centers on elevating building performance across a diverse portfolio. As a Sr Sustainability Specialist, you will contribute to projects spanning healthcare facilities, corporate offices, laboratories, museums, local, state, and federal government buildings, higher‑education campuses, and major infrastructure improvements. The ideal candidate brings experience in sustainable design and certification administration. This role requires working in person and/or on‑site three days per week, with the option to work remotely for the remaining two days. In this role you will: Expand HGA’s LCA services through building strategic partnerships with clients alongside our principals and business developers. Provide recommendations to project teams on low-GWP products, structural/material optimization, and procurement strategies. Refine and streamline LCA workflow through the development of LCA tools, templates, and other resources to ensure high quality analysis and reports are consistently delivered by the national team. Perform WBLCA in partnership with design teams. Ability to effectively communicate complex climate mitigation and GHG emissions concepts, in written and oral form, to a diverse range of professionals including clients and multi-disciplinary project teams to give everyone a common understanding. Maintain expertise in whole building life cycle assessment methodologies (e.g. ISO14040/44, EN 15978). Provide sustainability leadership on projects, including: Leading visioning/goal setting workshops and sustainable design charrettes with project teams and clients; Advising PMs and Principals on staffing to best leverage in-house experts to achieve the project’s sustainability goals; Sustainability and certification project management, including completing certification credit forms and supporting documentation; and Design team coordination to execute sustainability goals, including providing education and guidance to support discipline credit documentation. Additional skills: Excellent written & verbal communication skills and experience communicating with technical disciplines. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail. Ability to apply intensive and diversified knowledge of principles and practices to broad assignments and devise new approaches to problems encountered. Experience facilitating project visioning and goal setting workshops and developing and implementing sustainability goals and targets on projects. Balances understanding of the details with high level visioning and goal setting. Excellent people skills and the ability to coordinate and collaborate with various disciplines and stakeholders. Open and collaborative communication style. Ability to multitask and manage time efficiently across multiple projects. Meet deadlines and be flexible to changing deadlines. Well-developed interpersonal skills, maturity, credibility, confidence, and integrity. Focused and highly self-motivated. Able to work independently as well as in close collaboration with other staff members and multiple client project team. Exercises skills of persuasion and negotiation on critical issues. Maintains awareness of risk management for potential project issues and involves PIC/PM if needed. Able to teach and mentor team members with different expertise to support knowledge-sharing and elevating the firm’s overall expertise in sustainable design. Experience with environmental product declaration and product category rules. You will need to have: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Architecture, Interior Design, Engineering, Building Science, the Built Environment, Sustainability, or a related field; alternatively, demonstrated competency through a different degree combined with substantial sustainable design experience. Background in architecture or structural engineering strongly preferred. 8+ years of experience in design and construction project delivery preferred. Minimum 5 years of experience leading sustainability strategy for buildings - hands-on work with life cycle assessment (LCA) and embodied carbon analysis. Solid understanding of environmental and sustainable business practices, relevant regulatory frameworks, and current industry trends. Strong expertise in integrating embodied carbon assessment and reduction strategies into standard design and construction workflows and timelines. Deep knowledge of sustainable construction practices, low‑carbon materials, and material transparency resources such as EPDs, HPDs, and Declare labels. Proficiency with embodied carbon tools used in project delivery (e.g., One Click LCA, EC3) is essential. Advanced familiarity with embodied carbon and whole‑building life cycle assessment (WBLCA) concepts required. Experience with design and analysis software such as Rhino, Grasshopper, Radiance, DIVA, Revit, AutoCAD, SketchUp, Sefaira, and Insight360 preferred. Ability to read and interpret architectural and engineering drawings, specifications, and technical design documents. LEED Accredited Professional with experience supporting LEED‑certified projects. Demonstrated ability to incorporate the AIA Framework for Design Excellence into the design process. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $104,400 - $136,000 depending on qualifications, experience and location . Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Qualifications include: • Strong customer service skills. • Ability to handle multi-task environment and medical emergencies. • Basic PC skills such as Microsoft Word and department specific software programs. • Ability to communicate verbally in good English. • Graduate of an accredited School of Pharmacy (BS/PharmD degree) • Current Maryland Pharmacist license required • Strongly Preferred: Minimum one year of hospital pharmacy practice • Considered: Recent graduate w/ minimum 2 years hospital pharmacy technician/intern experience; or non-hospital pharmacist experience, with counseling and medication therapy management • Supervisory/management experience not required but welcome • Strongly Preferred: ASHP accredited PGY-1 Residency completion • Strongly Preferred: Board of Pharmacy Specialties (BPS) Certification • Strongly Preferred: American Heart Association Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) Certification • Strongly Preferred: Member of American Society of Health-system Pharmacists (ASHP), and/or Maryland Society of Health-system Pharmacist (MSHP) Work Schedule: Employment Type: Full time Hours per Week: 40 hrs/week Typical Daily Schedule: e.g., 6am-2:30pm; 7am-3:30pm; 8am-4:30pm; 10am-6:30pm; 1:30pm-10pm; 2pm - 10:30pm Monday through Friday Shift Type: Rotating Day/Evening. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is a flexible one, working a minimum of 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays the ideal candidate will be able to consider further days up to 40 hrs per week with notice. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. This role will be flexible in as much as the minimum hrs a week will be 24 but from time to time we may seek additional hrs up to and including 40 hrs per week. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. £13.45 per hour + bonus + company benefits.

description: Base Salary Range*: $185,000 to $230,000 *The salary range above is an estimated annual base salary for a full-time position. Your Total Rewards would include a base salary plus performance-based incentives and other valuable benefits. Our compensation models utilize fair market value data specific to your specialty. Position Summary MemorialCare Heart & Vascular Institute is seeking to add an experienced, board-certified Physician Assistant (PA) to its cardiovascular surgery team at MemorialCare Orange Coast Medical Center in beautiful southern California (Fountain Valley - Orange County). The selected PA will be employed by MemorialCare Medical Group, a for-profit multi-specialty group practice affiliated with MemorialCare Medical Foundation. Job Duties and Responsibilities The PA-Cardiac Surgery assists the supervising surgeon in the operating room, but also plays an integral role in the global management of patients undergoing cardiothoracic, vascular, or aortic surgery. Includes all aspects of peri-operative care from performing patient histories and daily rounds to invasive surgical procedures, including the critical retrieval of vascular tissue in coronary bypass procedures Competitive candidates must have extensive knowledge of artificial cardiac devices, ventilators and the pharmacologic effects of vasoactive/cardiotonic drugs The PA also provides patient care expertise in the postoperative acute care units, step-down units and regular floors First assist in the O.R., participate in aortic clinic, endovascular vein harvesting Be part of one of the larger programs on the west coast for comprehensive aortic diagnosis and treatment Financial & Benefits Competitive salary and annual incentive bonus plan Full and comprehensive benefits for provider and family include: Medical, dental, vision, life insurance, disability, 401k retirement plan with employer contribution, and more Medical license and DEA renewal reimbursement CME reimbursement fund Generous PTO plan MemorialCare Medical Foundation is a physician-based organization with more than 2,000 employed and affiliated physicians in Orange and Los Angeles counties. MemorialCare Health System is listed among the top 20 percent of health systems nationwide by Thomson Reuters, one of the top 100 integrated healthcare networks nationwide, and the top 10 in the west region by IMS. MemorialCare Health System is the predominant fully integrated health system in the Southern Los Angeles County and Orange County with over 200 locations.

title: Stationary Engineer, Variable Part-Time Rotating Shifts, Plant Operations
description: Shady Grove Medical Center If you are a current Adventist HealthCare employee, please click this link to apply through your Workday account. Shady Grove Medical Center seeks to hire a Stationary Engineer who will embrace our mission to extend God’s care through the ministry of physical, mental and spiritual healing. As a Stationary Engineer you will: • Be responsible for maintaining and controlling all plant equipment including boilers, chillers, pumps, cooling towers, and all associated equipment. • Maintain, service, and repair all boilers, chillers, generators, and associated equipment daily • Coordinate outside contractor support, as needed, or assist in facilitating the completion of repairs and projects too large to be done in-house. • Obtain appropriate approval from leadership and properly document transactions • Complete assigned monthly PM work orders on time; document all work performed in the CMMS computer database • Record data such as temperature, hours of operation, fuel consumption, temperature or pressure, water levels, voltage load, and generator balance; records operation and maintenance actions taken during shift in the operator’s logbook or through the electronic system(s) Qualifications include: • High School Diploma or GED required • 3 - 5 years of experience in commercial or hospital central utility plant operations required • 1st Class Stationary Engineer License State of Maryland • Valid US Driver’s License Required • Strong knowledge of boiler safety and power distribution • Strong verbal and written communication skills • Ability to read blueprints • Demonstrated mechanical aptitude • Ability to prioritize and remain calm in demanding situations • Able to use hand and power tools Work Schedule: Employment Type: Variable Part-Time Hours per Week: 19 hrs/week Typical Daily Schedule: Variable (630a-3pm) (230p-11pm) (10p-630a) Shift Type: Rotating Weekend Requirements: Required as needed (12-hour shifts) 630p-7a Holiday Requirements: Required/On-call/Follow company holiday calendar On-Call: no Shift Differential Eligible: Yes Pay Range: $27.70 - $40.16 If the salary range is not displayed or if the position is Per Diem (with a fixed rate), salary discussions will take place during the screening process. Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), this position is classified as: United States of America (Non-Exempt) At Adventist HealthCare our job is to care for you. We do this by offering: Work life balance through nonrotating shifts Recognition and rewards for professional expertise 403(b) retirement plan Free Employee parking Employee Assistance Program (EAP) support As a faith-based organization, with over a century of caring for the communities in the Maryland area, Adventist HealthCare has earned a reputation for high-quality, compassionate care. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). Equal Employment Opportunity Adventist HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We are committed to attracting, engaging, and developing the best people to cultivate our mission-centric culture. Our goal is to have a welcoming, equitable, and safe place to work and grow for all employees, no matter their background. AHC does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy and related medical conditions, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Adventist HealthCare will make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.  Adventist HealthCare is a religious organization as defined under applicable law; however, it will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations for applicants’ religious beliefs. Applicants who wish to request accommodations for disabilities or religious belief should contact the Support Center HR Office.

title: Team Leader - Meals
description: We’re the leading food producer for the health and social care sector, providing delicious, nutritious and sustainable meals for people at home or in care. We’re also the parent company of Wiltshire Farm Foods, the UK’s premier home-delivery service for ready-made meals. Through our wide range of well-presented, nutritious meals to suit every taste and dietary need, we aim to make a real difference to lives of elderly and vulnerable people in care. We’re proud to be a Living Wage employer and member of the Ethical Trading Initiative. We support British food and farming and operate a successful programme of waste reduction and resource efficiency across our whole business. In addition to your salary you will receive a comprehensive training program, 25 days annual leave holiday entitlement, the ability to buy additional annual leave, retail discounts, free car parking, subsidised staff restaurant, free turkey (or voucher) at Christmas and much more ! The department and team The main purpose of the Team Leader (Grade 6) is to lead, motivate, develop and encourage our team to deliver the daily production targets to an agreed standard. £17.10 per hour + Bonus + Benefits

title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is working 20 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. £12.36 per hour from 1st January 2025 (£12.60 per hour from 1st May 2025) + bonus + company benefits.

title: Coldstore Operative
description: Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. T he ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceeds expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. The cold store runs at a chilly -20 degrees centigrade but don’t worry, protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. Reporting to the Driver Supervisor and Senior Freezer Operative, you are responsible for helping to pack customers frozen orders ready for dispatch in a timely and accurate manner. £13.05 per hour + bonus + company benefits.

title: Senior Product Developer
description: Can you bring exciting food to life and help create market‑leading meals and desserts? If you are inspired by all things food, thrive on solving problems, and get excited about turning ideas into reality, our Senior Product Developer role is for you. We are seeking an experienced Product Developer to join our passionate team of chefs, technologists and dietitians who deliver award‑winning innovation, acting as a development lead on one of our market leading business units across key projects and programmes. This is a role where creativity meets technical expertise and where, as the lead developer, your work truly makes a difference to our customers across the UK. You will play a pivotal part in leading and driving the Wiltshire Farm Foods product pipeline, improving current favourites, and pushing the boundaries of what great food can be. This is a hands‑on leadership role, where you will take ownership for delivering exceptional development projects and programmes from concept through to launch. You will confidently navigate the stage‑gate process, ensuring every product meets our standards, policies and customer expectations, while guiding and influencing stakeholders throughout development. You’ll identify emerging trends and opportunities, pitch exciting new ideas to the business, work closely with our Category Development team, and lead projects that enhance our customers’ experience and deliver competitive advantage. This role is based at our UK Headquarters in Trowbridge Wiltshire.

title: Transport & Operational Graduate
description: apetito are looking for a Transport & Operational Graduate to join the Distribution, Warehouse and Customer Service business unit based in Portbury, Bristol. We are seeking a dynamic and motivated individual to join our Warehouse and Transport team in an exciting rotational programme designed for growth and development into a management role. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone eager to learn and progress in a fast-paced, hands-on environment. As part of this role, you will rotate between various departments and key functions, gaining exposure to all aspects of warehouse operations, transport logistics, and customer service. Flexibility, adaptability, and a passion for leadership are essential as you grow with us and develop the skills necessary to step into a managerial role. We will create you a plan which will rotate you between customer service team, stock team, planning, driver +  Fleet Management and Warehousing. We will also be looking for you to focus on some specific projects as you complete these rotations. You will be reporting into the Warehouse manager you will work alongside all the senior management for the division. The operation is based at a National Distribution Centre, this incorporates a Frozen Storage and Order Fulfilment centre that operates 24 hours a day from Sunday Afternoon to Friday Night.  The core hours of operation for this role will be between 06:00 to 22:00 (Monday to Friday) in 8 hour shifts, with occasional requirement to work at night. This role will provide a graduate with: - Experience in a FMCG temperature controlled distribution operation - Exposure to the senior management team. - Opportunity to interact with and experience all parts of a business - Involvement with specific projects that they will be able to co-ordinate and play an active part in - The opportunity to develop general business skills and relationships

title: Continuous Improvement Manager
description: Are you a passionate change agent who thrives on unlocking efficiencies and empowering teams? We’re seeking a results-driven Continuous Improvement Manager to elevate our national frozen food distribution network. You’ll deploy advanced Lean tools and Six Sigma methodologies to transform warehouse and transport performance—delivering tangible value across safety, service, cost, and quality. This is more than a process role—it’s about building a culture of relentless improvement at the heart of one of the UK’s most vital frozen supply chains. This role is based at our UK Distribution Centre in Portbury on the outskirts of Bristol.

title: Senior PR Executive
description: As Senior PR Executive, you will play a key role in delivering high-quality, impactful PR activity that strengthens apetito’s reputation across our priority markets. You will need a high degree of autonomy and judgement, and be trusted to lead the delivery of high-profile PR activity under the direction of the PR Manager. Sitting within the central Brand & Communications team, you will work closely with colleagues across the business and external partners to bring our stories to life, ensuring our voice is credible, consistent and values-led. You will also act as a role model within the PR team, helping to maintain high standards and support the development of others. Most importantly, you will help share how apetito and Wiltshire Farm Foods are making a real difference to customers, communities and colleagues every single day. At apetito | Wiltshire Farm Foods, we are on an ambitious journey. With bold growth targets ahead, how we tell our story and protect our reputation will play a critical role in helping us succeed. We are looking for someone with experience in PR and media relations, able to craft compelling stories as well as demonstrate the commercial impact of PR delivery. This role is based in our Trowbridge head office and you will need to be on site at least 3 days per week.

title: Catering Account Manager
description: We’re looking for a hands-on, driven individual with a passion for food, service, and people. This role would suit someone from a catering, contract catering, or events background—whether that’s a Chef, Area Support Manager, or an experienced operator ready to step into a more commercial, client-facing role. If you’re someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, takes pride in delivering great service, and is motivated by making a real difference to customers and teams, this could be the perfect next step for you. As a Catering Account Manager, you’ll play a key role in strengthening client relationships, supporting both existing and new business, and bringing our “Passion for Service” to life. The focus is on driving retention, supporting growth, and ensuring our offer remains relevant, engaging, and consistently delivered to a high standard. The ideal candidate will be based in or around Northampton, with the ability to cover a territory spanning Northamptonshire and the M1 corridor across the Midlands and South of England, enabling effective customer engagement and regular on-site presence. 4 days per week will be spent in the field with customers with 1 days working from home. Some overnight travel will be required. Due to the nature of this role an Enhanced DBS will be required.

Perform SBAR and participate in rounds in ICU and NICU. • Manage patients’ airways for spontaneous breathing and respiratory compromised patients. • Perform quality control and maintenance on assigned equipment. • Execute all other duties as required. Qualifications include: • Graduate of AMA approved respiratory program • AS in Respiratory Therapy required • 2 years (NICU experience for NICU 2791) preferred • Registered Respiratory Therapist by NBRC • Active Maryland State license by the Department of Health and Mental Hygiene. Must obtain an Advanced Certification within the 1st year of employment. • Willing to obtain an advanced certification when eligible. Work Schedule: Employment Type: Per Diem Hours per Week: 12 hrs Typical Daily Schedule: 7a-7p Shift Type: Day Weekend Requirements: none Holiday Requirements: one major and one minor On-Call: no Shift Differential Eligible: Yes This is a per diem position with a rate of $45/hour Pay Range: If the salary range is not displayed or if the position is Per Diem (with a fixed rate), salary discussions will take place during the screening process. Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), this position is classified as: United States of America (Non-Exempt) At Adventist HealthCare our job is to care for you. We do this by offering: Work life balance through nonrotating shifts Recognition and rewards for professional expertise 403(b) retirement plan Free Employee parking Employee Assistance Program (EAP) support As a faith-based organization, with over a century of caring for the communities in the Maryland area, Adventist HealthCare has earned a reputation for high-quality, compassionate care. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). Equal Employment Opportunity Adventist HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We are committed to attracting, engaging, and developing the best people to cultivate our mission-centric culture. Our goal is to have a welcoming, equitable, and safe place to work and grow for all employees, no matter their background. AHC does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy and related medical conditions, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Adventist HealthCare will make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.  Adventist HealthCare is a religious organization as defined under applicable law; however, it will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations for applicants’ religious beliefs. Applicants who wish to request accommodations for disabilities or religious belief should contact the Support Center HR Office.

title: NP- Adult Home Visit Program - F/T Orange County - Remote
description: Summary: MemorialCare Medical Group is seeking to add an outstanding Nurse Practitioner (NP) to its expanding Adult Home Visit Program in South Orange County. The program provides medical and palliative care to patients experiencing complex health challenges who are currently unable to visit their primary care physician (PCP). The program's goal is to provide timely, focused care that improves patient health and quality of life. This home visit program is coordinated on behalf of the patient’s PCP in the home, board-and-care, or assisted living setting. The NP will be part of an amazing interdisciplinary team and will receive assistance and work closely with other programs/departments. MemorialCare Medical Group is a physician-based organization with more than 2,000 employed and affiliated physicians in Orange and Los Angeles counties. MemorialCare Health System is listed among the top 20 percent of health systems nationwide by Thomson Reuters, one of the top 100 integrated healthcare networks nationwide, and the top 10 in the west region by IMS. MemorialCare Health System is the predominant fully integrated health system in the Southern Los Angeles County and Orange County with over 200 locations. Essential Functions and Responsibilities of the Job In this full-time role, you’ll be working independently to manage and coordinate medically complex patients with regards to the following: Provide history and physical as appropriate for each patient Effectively identify, evaluate, and address acute and chronic health conditions as well as disease prevention and health promotion issues Ability to perform many procedures, including wound closure, I&D, foreign body removal, etc. In the near future, may include providing palliative care in rotating clinic locations 10-20% of the time. Maintains legible, accurate and confidential medical records. Documents all medical evaluations, diagnoses, procedures, treatments, outcomes, education, referrals and consultation Coordinate and direct appropriate care for patient by means of an initial diagnosis and treatment, obtaining second opinions as necessary and consultation with contracted specialists Follow-up on referrals made to specialists to assess the results of the care, medication regimen and special treatment and ensure continuous care Establish procedures to contact patients when they miss appointments, require re-scheduling for additional visits, or confirming referrals to a specialist for care Coordinate patient referrals for other service Submit services with the usual and customary billed charges in a timely fashion, knowledgeable about HCC coding + Responsible for a mental health assessment, ruling out mental disorders due to a general medical condition, ruling out substance related disorders and identifying general medical conditions that cause or exacerbate psychological symptoms Establish and maintain effective relationships with members and their caregivers in collaboration with clinical staff, PCP’s, specialists, Social Workers, Case Managers, etc. Identify the appropriate level of care, i.e., hospitalization, home health, palliative care or hospice. Participate in clinical and operational data collection to demonstrate the impact of the program. Communicate with patients, families, and interdisciplinary teams around goals of care and advanced care planning. Experience/Qualifications/Certifications: 2-3 years NP experience in acute care, primary care, prior home health experience + Master’s Degree in Nursing (required) Active RN license in the State of California (required) Current BLS certification (required) Current Nurse Practitioner certification in the State of California (required) Current DEA license Bilingual in Spanish+ Financial & Benefits Mileage reimbursement Full and Comprehensive Benefits for Provider and Family Benefits to include: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Disability, Malpractice Insurance and Tail coverage 401k retirement plan with employer contribution 529 College Savings plan Generous PTO plan Base salary range: $131,000-$160,000+* *The salary range above is an estimated annual base salary for a full-time position and depending on experience. Our compensation model utilizes fair market value data specific to your specialty.

Sr Marketing Coordinator
description: Ready to advance your marketing career - join HGA's Marketing team! Locations: Minneapolis, MN; Milwaukee, WI; Boston MA; Washington, DC; Santa Monica, San Francisco, or Sacramento, CA As a nationally recognized architecture, engineering, and planning firm, we are seeking a Senior Marketing Coordinator to support our marketing efforts within the Healthcare sector . If you're a creative, results-driven marketer who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, we want to hear from you! This individual will own the end-to-end process of producing marketing collateral, coordinating field marketing, and developing HGA stories to inspire action. From brainstorming ideas and writing content to coordinating / producing proposals and award submittals, we’re looking for someone who can help us target and win more specific clients and projects. In this role you will: Lead the end-to-end process – project management, content development, and document production – for marketing collateral for client prepositioning and pursuits, specifically: RFQ/P Responses including qualifications packages, proposals, and interviews that integrate creative storytelling and visual delivery. Collaborate with Healthcare practice group leaders, principals, and business developers to implement messaging strategy, define narrative approach, and write/proof copy for marketing content. Develop high-quality marketing content and collateral to support other marketing initiatives including project awards submissions, conferences, social media, speaking engagements, and publication advertising. Coordinate external (events, conferences, photoshoots) marketing activities and produce corresponding marketing collateral. Work in a highly interactive and cross-functional position, collaborating and cultivating positive relationships with multiple team members across various teams and offices. Partner with the Marketing and Communications team on the execution of strategic initiatives and champion marketing projects across teams as appropriate. Take initiative with a solution-focused mindset with an ability to work in ambiguity and pivot through the unexpected. You will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Art History (or other related field) or a relevant combination of experience, education, and training 8+ years of professional experience writing marketing content, creating marketing collateral in Adobe InDesign, and managing multiple marketing projects from beginning to end Experience managing and producing RFQ/P responses in the architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) industry preferred, ideally with a Healthcare focus Exceptional aptitude in Adobe InDesign and proficiency with other Adobe Creative Suite programs (Photoshop, Illustrator) Proficiency in Adobe Workfront, Canva, and/or VantagePoint is a plus Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams) Proofreading, editing and standardizing proposal text for proper grammar, spelling, comprehension and style while adhering to brand standardizations High attention to detail is critical Ability to work in a team and independently, self-starter and able to multitask workload to meet deadlines Mentorship experience Knowledge of Healthcare industry; building types, common healthcare verbiage, etc. Salary information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted . Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $89,300 - $106,000 (Mid-West) or $97,100 - $126,200 (East and West Coast) depending on qualifications and experience. Additional information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects and engineers—and help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

description: Thoracic Surgeon, MemorialCare Medical Group Primary Location: MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center – Long Beach, CA Additional Practice Sites: Orange Coast Medical Center & Saddleback Medical Center MemorialCare Heart and Vascular Institute is seeking a Board Certified or Board Eligible, robotic-trained, Thoracic Surgeon to join our well-established, physician-led medical group in Southern California. The primary clinical practice will be based at MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center, with opportunities to perform thoracic surgical cases at Orange Coast Medical Center and Saddleback Medical Center as part of our coordinated, multi-campus care model. Position Highlights Employed physician position with MemorialCare Medical Group Integrated care environment with access to advanced surgical technology and multidisciplinary teams Opportunity to serve a diverse patient population across multiple MemorialCare campuses Collegial, quality-focused culture with strong clinical and operational support Position Responsibilities The Thoracic Surgeon will provide comprehensive inpatient and outpatient surgical care for patients with diseases and disorders of the thoracic cavity. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Evaluate, diagnose, and manage conditions affecting the lungs, pleura, mediastinum, esophagus, diaphragm, and chest wall Perform a full range of general thoracic surgical procedures, including both open and minimally invasive techniques (VATS and/or robotic; 60-70% of our volume is robotic), such as lung resections, lobectomies, mediastinal mass resections, and esophageal procedures Conduct thorough pre‑operative assessments, including review of diagnostic imaging and test results (e.g., CT scans, PET scans, pulmonary function tests), and formulate individualized surgical treatment plans Provide postoperative management and follow‑up care, including monitoring recovery, managing complications, and coordinating ongoing care across the continuum Collaborate closely with multidisciplinary teams, including pulmonology, oncology, gastroenterology, radiology, anesthesia, critical care, and nursing, to ensure high‑quality, coordinated patient care Participate in inpatient consults, surgical call coverage, and rounding responsibilities at MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center, with additional surgical coverage at Orange Coast Medical Center and Saddleback Medical Center as clinically indicated Educate patients and their families regarding diagnoses, surgical options, procedural risks and benefits, and postoperative recovery expectations in a clear and compassionate manner Maintain accurate, timely, and complete medical documentation in compliance with MemorialCare policies, regulatory requirements, and quality standards Support quality improvement initiatives, peer review activities, and evidence‑based practice guidelines to enhance patient outcomes and safety Stay current with advancements in thoracic surgery techniques, technology, and best practices through continuing medical education and professional development Qualifications Board Certified or Board Eligible in Thoracic Surgery Active California medical license (or ability to obtain) Eligible to work in the United States Commitment to clinical excellence, patient-centered care, and collaboration within a multidisciplinary team Compensation & Benefits Competitive compensation package Shareholder / Partner eligibility after two years of employment Comprehensive benefits package for Physician and family Benefits include: Medical, dental, vision, life Insurance, disability, 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching, 529 College Savings plan, generous PTO plan Relocation assistance Professional liability insurance About MemorialCare Medical Group MemorialCare Medical Group is a physician-led, not-for-profit medical group aligned with MemorialCare Medical Foundation within MemorialCare Health System. We are dedicated to delivering high-quality, evidence-based care while fostering physician engagement, professional growth, and work-life balance. description: JOB DESCRIPTION: About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader, creating breakthrough science to improve people’s health. A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. The Opportunity This position works out of our Cairo Office in the Established Pharmaceutical Division where we are committed to bringing the benefits of our trusted medicines to more people in the world’s fastest-growing countries. Our broad portfolio of high-quality and differentiated branded generic medicines reaches across multiple therapeutic areas including gastroenterology, women's health, cardiometabolic, pain management/central nervous system, and respiratory. As a Product Manager, you’ll have the chance to lead a group of initiatives and campaign, driven by insights and based on strategic planning and financial acumen. You’ll manage the brand equity, collaborate cross-fucntionally and influence decision-makers for innovative initiatives to grow the brands and manage their life cycle. What You’ll Do: Develops annual TA strategies and brand plans in coordination with the country’s relevant cross-functional stakeholders. Develops annual & quarterly tactical plan of activities for relevant brands, in coordination with Sales & Marketing stakeholders. Align with regional & global marketing teams on brands strategies & updates, while insuring adherence to global brands strategy implementation. Plan and execute HCPs’ engagement educational programs & activities to raise the awareness about brands & relevant therapeutic areas. Train sales force on product knowledge and brand strategies. Generate and Analyze market insight & researches to optimize the decision-making process. Relevant KOL management in coordination with relevant commercial partners. Integral member of the demand and supply review, and active participation in forecasting and market projections. Regularly track the execution tactical plans and the impact of activities on overall performance, optimize ROI. Manage contacts and ensure strategic alliance between Abbott functional teams (Finance, Demand & Logistics management, Distribution, Sales & Marketing team), through building a strong internal network and providing business rationale, reach consensus, resolve conflicts, escalate issues. Regularly collect and provide Management with information about marketing & commercial activities of other pharmaceutical companies and identify market trends. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in pharmaceutical sciences. Minimum 6 years of experience in a commercial role in the pharma industry. Minimum 2 years of experience in a marketing role in the pharma industry. Excellent communication skills with high proficiency in spoken and written English & Arabic. Proficient MS office tools (PowerPoint & Excel), and fair understanding of digital promotion tools. Remarkable KOLs’ engagement record. Thinks strategically and demonstrates learning agility, with a growth mindset. Good financial and business acumen. Preferred Qualifications People management experience. MBA or a marketing post-graduate degree. Proven ability to influence cross-functional teams, internal and external stakeholders. Apply Now Follow your career aspirations to Abbott for diverse opportunities with a company that can help you build your future and live your best life. title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

description: Lead Innovative Electrical Design at HGA - Washington, DC or Boston, MA! Bring your passion and expertise in government, commercial, corporate, higher education, and/or performing arts projects to HGA , an award-winning, integrated architecture, engineering, and planning firm. As a Senior Electrical Project Engineer , you will play a pivotal role in leading and managing large-scale electrical projects and teams—locally and nationally. We’re looking for a highly organized, communicative, and effective team player with a passion for high-quality design and innovative problem-solving . If you thrive on leading teams, ensuring technical excellence, and delivering impactful designs, this is an exciting opportunity to grow your career. What You’ll Do: Lead electrical design on large-scale projects across various sectors, including corporate , higher education , and performing arts. Manage project teams locally and nationally to ensure high-quality, timely delivery of electrical systems and solutions. Develop and review electrical designs, including power distribution, lighting systems, and control systems, ensuring compliance with codes and client requirements. Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior engineers and design professionals. Collaborate with architects, engineers, and other stakeholders to integrate electrical systems into innovative, functional designs. Communicate effectively with clients, consultants, and project teams to drive projects forward and ensure expectations are met. In this role you will: Assess the requirements of a project, break a project into tasks, and work with the design team to determine scope, budget, and staffing. Write scopes of work for electrical design based on preliminary review and meetings with the client. Prepare engineering studies and schematic design for new systems and analyze operation of existing systems. Present complex engineering analysis clearly and effectively to owner and design teams. Document existing conditions and perform complex and detailed troubleshooting. Resolve a variety of complex problems, i.e. conflicting design requirements, code issues, varying design options, and difficult condition requirements. Research non-routine code issues and obtain interpretations from code officials. Produce sketches and system schematics for complicated design tasks. Prepare engineering opinion of probable cost for electrical systems and review contractor cost estimates. Develop calculations using engineering formulas and skills for Electrical designs such as whole building electrical loads, lighting calculations, power distribution calculations, equipment sizing, editing specification sections, and creating punch lists. Review drawings and documents to assure compliance with company design and production standards. Assure that drawings and designs are in accordance with design standards. Develop and maintain a thorough understanding of construction processes and delivery methods. Assist in the education and development of design staff and act as a resource for questions. You will need to have: BS or MS degree in electrical engineering. Professional licensure is required. 12+ years of experience in leading design of government, commercial, corporate, higher education, and/or performing arts project. Documented experience with an MEP engineering firm or A/E firm providing electrical design. Expertise in National Fire Protection Association Codes (NFPA) and an understanding of applicable (city, state, federal) requirements and pertinent standards is essential. Active government security clearance, or eligibility to obtain and maintain the required clearance for project assignments. Working knowledge of Revit software. Familiarity with electrical analysis software (SKM, Easy Power) and knowledge of the current sustainable technologies applicable to the design of commercial electrical systems. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary range:  $121.000 - $147,800 depending on experience and expertise. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.
